title: Stelis croatii
intro: Stelis croatii, also known as Croat's orchid, is a species of orchid that grows in the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, as well as its potential to aid in the treatment of various diseases such as cancer and diabetes. The bioactive constituents of Stelis croatii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which have been identified as responsible for its therapeutic properties. In traditional medicine, Stelis croatii is often prepared into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat a range of health issues, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
taxonomy: Stelis croatii is a species of orchid in the genus Stelis. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. Stelis croatii is further classified as a member of the subtribe Pleurothallidinae, within the tribe Epidendreae. The subfamily Pleurothallidinae is known to contain various epiphytic orchids, which are plants that grow on other plants for support. Stelis croatii is a relatively specific and unique species within the broader classification of orchids.
